domains.name: Data & Privacy
domains.description: Protect against data leakage, IP leakage, and training on user data without consent

controls.title: Establish input data policy
controls.description: Establish and communicate AI input data policies covering how customer data is used for model training, inference processing, data retention periods, and customer data rights.

activities.description: Define and communicate input data usage policies including how customer data is used for inference and model training, establishing data retention periods, and documenting customer data rights.
Implement technical controls to enforce data retention and deletion policies through automating data deletion based on retention schedules.
Document processes for handling end-user data subject rights including opt-in/opt-out rights, access, portability, or deletion of input data.

controls.title: Establish output data policy
controls.description: Establish AI output ownership, usage, opt-out and deletion policies to customers and communicate these policies.

activities.description: Establishing output ownership and usage rights policies, specifying customer ownership of AI-generated outputs, defining permitted uses, documenting usage restrictions, and disclosing opt-out and deletion procedures.

controls.title: Limit AI agent data collection
controls.description: Implement safeguards to limit AI agent data access to task-relevant information based on user roles and context.

activities.description: Configuring data collection limits to reduce data and privacy exposure, limiting data collection to task-relevant information based on context.
Deploying monitoring mechanisms ensuring AI systems only perform necessary inference and logging deviations from defined operational scope.
Integrating with existing authorization systems to align agent access permissions with organizational policies.

controls.title: Protect IP & trade secrets
controls.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent AI systems from leaking company intellectual property or confidential information.

activities.description: Providing user guidance on protecting confidential information.
Leveraging foundation model provider protections with zero data retention policies.
Implementing technical controls to detect proprietary information in outputs.
Establishing output monitoring for high-risk IP scenarios.

controls.title: Prevent cross-customer data exposure
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent cross-customer data exposure when combining customer data from multiple sources.

activities.description: Establishing explicit consent and disclosure for combined data usage.
Implementing customer data isolation controls with strict logical and physical separation.
Implementing privacy-enhancing technologies to reduce competitive exposure.

controls.title: Prevent PII leakage
controls.description: Establish safeguards to prevent personal data leakage through AI outputs and logs.

activities.description: Implementing safeguards to prevent personal data leakage through AI system outputs and logs, filtering prompts and outputs for personal identifiers.
Requiring authentication and authorization for PII access with role-based access controls.
Integrating with existing data loss prevention (DLP) systems to monitor and block outputs containing personal data.

controls.title: Prevent IP violations
controls.description: Implement safeguards and technical controls to prevent AI outputs from violating copyrights, trademarks, or other third-party intellectual property rights.

activities.description: Documenting foundation model provider IP protections including indemnification clauses.
Establishing supplementary content filtering mechanisms for IP screening.
Implementing user guidance and guardrails to reduce IP risk.

domains.name: Security
domains.description: Protect AI systems against adversarial attacks, unauthorized access, and data exposure

controls.title: Third-party testing of adversarial robustness
controls.description: Implement adversarial testing program to validate system resilience against adversarial inputs and prompt injection attempts.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report demonstrating adversarial robustness testing including risk taxonomy, methodology, findings, and remediation timelines.
Penetration test reports incorporating AI-specific test cases, shared threat models, and testing calendars.

controls.title: Detect adversarial input
controls.description: Implement monitoring capabilities to detect and respond to adversarial inputs and prompt injection attempts.

activities.description: Implementing monitoring for prompt injection patterns, jailbreak techniques, adversarial input attempts.
Logging suspected adversarial attacks with relevant context and escalating based on severity.
Updating detection rules based on emerging adversarial techniques quarterly.
Implementing adversarial input detection prior to AI model processing.
Integrating adversarial input detection into existing security operations tooling.

controls.title: Manage public release of technical details
controls.description: Implement controls to prevent over-disclosure of technical information about AI systems.

activities.description: Policy document defining limitations and approval requirements for publicly sharing AI system technical details.
Approval records for public AI communications.

controls.title: Prevent AI endpoint scraping
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent probing or scraping of external AI endpoints.

activities.description: Implementing systems distinguishing between high-volume legitimate usage and adversarial behavior.
Implementing rate limiting and query restrictions.
Conducting simulated external attack testing of AI endpoints.
Tracking identified vulnerabilities and implementing protective measures.

controls.title: Implement real-time input filtering
controls.description: Implement real-time input filtering using automated moderation tools.

activities.description: Integration of automated moderation tools to filter inputs before reaching the foundation model.
Documentation explaining moderation approach including tool selection rationale and threshold settings.
User-facing communication when inputs are blocked.
Logging system capturing flagged inputs with metadata tracking.
Analysis of filter performance metrics with documented threshold adjustments.

controls.title: Prevent unauthorized AI agent actions
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent AI agents from performing actions beyond intended scope and authorized privileges.

activities.description: Restricting agent access to approved backend services and APIs, enforcing network segmentation or API gateway rules.
Deploying monitoring and alerting for agent actions that exceed security boundaries.

controls.title: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems
controls.description: Establish and maintain user access controls and admin privileges for AI systems in line with policy.

activities.description: System-level access controls tailored to AI systems using role-based or attribute-based access.
Conducting access reviews and updates at least quarterly.

controls.title: Protect model deployment environment
controls.description: Implement security measures for AI model deployment environments including encryption, access controls and authorization.

activities.description: Restricting access to production AI models based on job function.
Applying scoped API tokens, TLS for all endpoint traffic, schema validation.
Securing model hosting environments with minimal container images.
Verifying model integrity before deployment using cryptographic checksums.

controls.title: Limit output over-exposure
controls.description: Implement output limitations and obfuscation techniques to safeguard against information leakage.

activities.description: Reducing or limiting the number of results shown in outputs to relevant only.
Providing user-facing notices about output limitations.
Limiting the fidelity of model outputs with rounding, threshold bands, or obfuscation.

domains.name: Safety
domains.description: Prevent harmful AI outputs and brand risk through testing, monitoring and safeguards

controls.title: Define AI risk taxonomy
controls.description: Establish a risk taxonomy that categorizes risks within harmful, out-of-scope, and hallucinated outputs, tool calls, and other risks based on application-specific usage.

activities.description: Internal policy document or risk framework defining AI risk categories with severity levels and examples.
Quarterly reviews of risk taxonomy with review dates, participants, decisions, and version history.

controls.title: Conduct pre-deployment testing
controls.description: Conduct internal testing of AI systems prior to deployment across risk categories.

activities.description: Conducting pre-deployment testing with documented results, risk assessments, and approval sign-offs.
Integrating AI system testing into established SDLC gates.
Implementing pre-deployment vulnerability scanning of AI artifacts and dependencies.

controls.title: Prevent harmful outputs
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent harmful outputs including distressed outputs, angry responses, high-risk advice, offensive content, bias, and deception.

activities.description: Detecting and blocking harmful output types through content filtering systems.
Restricting high-risk recommendations in sensitive domains with disclaimers.
Monitoring for discriminatory patterns and implementing fairness checks.
Evaluating harm mitigation controls using performance metrics.

controls.title: Prevent out-of-scope outputs
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent out-of-scope outputs.

activities.description: Blocking rules, defensive prompting, or filtering for out-of-scope requests.
Logs showing out-of-scope attempts with frequency data and boundary updates.
User-facing guidance explaining system capabilities and limitations.

controls.title: Prevent customer-defined high risk outputs
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent additional high risk outputs as defined in risk taxonomy.

activities.description: Implementing detection and blocking mechanisms aligned with organizational risk taxonomy.
Establishing escalation procedures for flagged high-risk content.
Implementing automated real-time interventions.

controls.title: Prevent output vulnerabilities
controls.description: Implement safeguards to prevent security vulnerabilities in outputs from impacting users.

activities.description: Establishing output sanitization and validation procedures before presenting content to users.
Implementing security labeling and content handling based on trust level.
Detecting advanced output-based attack patterns.

controls.title: Flag high risk outputs
controls.description: Implement an alerting system that flags high-risk outputs for human review.

activities.description: Document or policy defining high-risk outputs requiring human review.
Detection code, configuration, or rules engine showing high-risk output filtering.
Workflow documentation for human review process for flagged outputs.

controls.title: Monitor AI risk categories
controls.description: Implement monitoring of AI systems across risk categories.

activities.description: Establish ongoing monitoring of AI outputs across risk categories through regular evaluations.
Maintain documentation recording identified risk scenarios with examples.
Integrate AI output monitoring with existing security tools.

controls.title: Enable real-time feedback and intervention
controls.description: Implement mechanisms to enable real-time user feedback collection and intervention mechanisms.

activities.description: Enable user intervention capabilities such as pause, stop, or redirect system behavior.
Review user feedback and intervention logs regularly.

controls.title: Third-party testing for harmful outputs
controls.description: Appoint expert third parties to evaluate system robustness to harmful outputs at least every 3 months.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report showing harmful output testing.

controls.title: Third-party testing for out-of-scope outputs
controls.description: Appoint expert third parties to evaluate system robustness to out-of-scope outputs at least every 3 months.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report showing out-of-scope output testing.

controls.title: Third-party testing for customer-defined risk
controls.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate system robustness to additional high-risk outputs as defined in risk taxonomy.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report showing testing of customer-defined risk.

domains.name: Reliability
domains.description: Ensure AI system outputs are accurate, grounded, and tool calls are safe

controls.title: Prevent hallucinated outputs
controls.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent hallucinated outputs.

activities.description: Implementing factual accuracy controls via fact-checking mechanisms, flagging uncertain responses.
Requiring citations for factual claims, implementing source reliability checks.
Maintaining uncertainty communication through confidence levels and disclaimers.

controls.title: Third-party testing for hallucinations
controls.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate hallucinated outputs at least every 3 months.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report showing hallucination testing results.

controls.title: Restrict unsafe tool calls
controls.description: Implement safeguards or technical controls to prevent tool calls in AI systems from executing unauthorized actions, accessing restricted information, or making decisions beyond their intended scope.

activities.description: Function call validation and authorization, restricting tool access to approved functions, validating parameters before execution.
Enforcing rate limits and transaction caps for autonomous tool use.
Execution monitoring and logging; tracking all tool calls, monitoring for unauthorized access attempts.
Requiring human approval for sensitive tool operations.
Reviewing patterns of AI tool usage; identifying anomalies, updating tool permissions.

controls.title: Third-party testing of tool calls
controls.description: Appoint expert third-parties to evaluate tool calls in AI systems at least every 3 months.

activities.description: Third-party evaluation report showing tool call testing results.

domains.name: Accountability
domains.description: Ensure AI systems have clear ownership, oversight, and governance processes

controls.title: AI failure plan for security breaches
controls.description: Document an AI failure plan addressing privacy and security breaches by assigning accountable owners and establishing notification and remediation procedures.

activities.description: Assign breach response lead and document incident response procedures.
Define procedures for customer communications, regulatory reporting, and vendor notifications.
Implement system freeze capabilities, vulnerability fixes, access control updates.
Establish guidance on preserving evidence for legal review.

controls.title: AI failure plan for harmful outputs
controls.description: Document AI failure plan for harmful AI outputs that cause significant customer harm.

activities.description: Implementing customer communication protocols and immediate mitigation steps.
Defining harmful output category definitions and external support engagement.

controls.title: AI failure plan for hallucinations
controls.description: Document AI failure plan for hallucinated AI outputs that cause substantial customer financial loss.

activities.description: Establishing compensation assessment procedures and remediation measures.
Defining hallucination incident types and external support coordination.

controls.title: Assign accountability
controls.description: Document which AI system changes require formal review or approval, assign a lead accountable for each, and document their approval.

activities.description: Documentation defining which AI system changes require approval with assigned leads, plus approval records.
Implementing code signing and verification processes for AI models and deployment artifacts.

controls.title: Assess cloud vs on-prem processing
controls.description: Establish criteria for selecting cloud provider and circumstances for on-premises processing.

activities.description: Risk assessment and decision record evaluating cloud vs. on-premises factors.

owasp_aivss: Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Risk

controls.title: Conduct vendor due diligence
controls.description: Establish AI vendor due diligence processes for foundation and upstream model providers.

activities.description: Vendor assessment records showing evaluation criteria, scoring, verification, and approval decisions.

owasp_aivss: Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Risk

controls.title: Document system change approvals
controls.description: RETIRED - Merged with E004 at Q1 2026 update.

activities.description: RETIRED - merged into E004.

controls.title: Review internal processes
controls.description: Establish regular internal reviews of key processes and document review records and approvals.

activities.description: Quarterly reviews of decision processes including AI system changes, foundational model selection.
Collect and implement external feedback on AI systems.

controls.title: Monitor third-party access
controls.description: Implement systems to monitor third-party access to AI systems.

activities.description: Configuring logging for third-party interactions with captured metadata.

owasp_aivss: Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Risk

controls.title: Establish AI acceptable use policy
controls.description: Establish and implement an AI acceptable use policy defining prohibited AI usage for end-users.

activities.description: Policy document defining acceptable and prohibited AI usage.
Implementation of detection and monitoring systems for AUP violations.
User-facing alerts when acceptable use policy is violated.
Real-time blocking/alerting capabilities and violation tracking.

controls.title: Record processing locations
controls.description: Document AI data processing locations covering geographic locations of processing and inference endpoints.

activities.description: Maintaining AI infrastructure location documentation.
Implementing transfer compliance procedures.

controls.title: Document regulatory compliance
controls.description: Document applicable AI laws and standards, required data protections, and strategies for compliance.

activities.description: Identify applicable regulations, document compliance procedures, and review every 6 months.

controls.title: Implement quality management system
controls.description: Establish a quality management system for AI systems proportionate to the size of the organization.

activities.description: Defining quality objectives, metrics, and risk management approach.
Establishing change management, approval processes.
Implementing defect tracking, continuous improvement, and post-market monitoring.
Establishing data management and record-keeping systems.
Documenting communication procedures with regulatory authorities.

owasp_aivss: Agent Supply Chain and Dependency Risk

controls.title: Share transparency reports
controls.description: RETIRED - Merged into E017 at Q1 2026 update.

activities.description: RETIRED - merged into E017.

controls.title: Log model activity
controls.description: Maintain logs of AI system processes, actions, and model outputs to support incident investigation, auditing, and explanation.

activities.description: Capture system activity details including inputs, processing steps, outputs, and metadata.
Implement storage with retention policies, access controls, and data sanitation.
Ensure logs are tamper-evident and independently verifiable.

controls.title: Implement AI disclosure mechanisms
controls.description: Implement clear disclosure mechanisms to inform users when they are interacting with AI systems.

activities.description: Display clear notices when users interact with AI chatbots.
Provide audio notifications at the beginning of voice calls.
Mark AI-generated content with metadata, watermarks, or labels.
Notify users when autonomous AI agents perform actions.
Systems respond to user questions about whether they are interacting with AI.

controls.title: Document system transparency policy
controls.description: Establish a system transparency policy and maintain a repository of model cards, datasheets, and interpretability reports.

activities.description: Policy document defining transparency documentation requirements.
Transparency documentation artifacts: model cards, datasheets, interpretability reports, AI Bill of Materials.
Policy defining transparency sharing practices with stakeholder categories.

domains.name: Society
domains.description: Prevent AI from enabling societal harm through cyberattacks or catastrophic misuse

controls.title: Prevent AI cyber misuse
controls.description: Implement or document guardrails to prevent AI-enabled misuse for cyber attacks and exploitation.

activities.description: Provider model cards and documentation describing offensive cyber capabilities and mitigation strategies.
Deploy content filtering to detect requests for malicious code generation and vulnerability exploitation.

controls.title: Prevent catastrophic misuse
controls.description: Implement or document guardrails to prevent AI-enabled catastrophic system misuse (chemical / bio / radio / nuclear).

activities.description: List of foundation models with CBRN capability information and risk evaluations.
Monitoring dashboard or alert configuration for catastrophic misuse patterns.
